DISCOVERY 302,THE COURT VACATED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING STATES THE FOLLOWING; DEFENDANT MARCUS MERCHASIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFFS RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY REQUESTS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS- SEVERAL DAYS AFTER THE STATUTORY DEADLINE (C.C.P. SECTION 1005), AND WITHOUT EXPLANATION FOR THIS DELAY, PLAINTIFF KONSTANTIN KUPFER FILED AN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION. THEREFORE, ON THE COURT'S MOTION, THE HEARING WILL BE MOVED TO FEBRUARY 23, 2017, TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH AMPLE TIME TO REPLY. JUDGE PRO TEM: MELINDA DERISH; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARCUS MERCHASIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 11, 2017. ON ITS OWN MOTION, THE COURT CONTINUES THE HEARING ANOTHER WEEK TO GIVE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO ENGAGE IN FURTHER MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. PER THE MARCH 30 EMAIL FROM MR. FELLNER, IT APPEARS THAT COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER DID NOT PARTICIPATE IN MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS AS REQUIRED BY THIS COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED IN ADVANCE OF THE MARCH 28 HEARING. IF COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER WAS NOT ABLE TO COMPLY WITH THAT TENTATIVE RULING, COUNSEL'S OBLIGATION WAS TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. FAILING TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING RESULTED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING BECOMING AN ORDER OF THE COURT, WHICH COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER DID NOT COMPLY WITH. GIVING COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER THE BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT, ANOTHER CONTINUANCE IS PROVIDED. NO FURTHER BENEFITS OF THE DOUBT WILL BE ACCORDED TO COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER ON THIS ISSUE. THE MEET AND CONFER MUST OCCUR NO LATER THAN APRIL 6. NO LATER THAN APRIL 7 COUNSEL FOR MR. MERCHASIN SHOULD SEND A REVISED REPORT BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR MR. KUPFER STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, WHAT ISSUES REMAIN IN DISPUTE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Set For Trial On Aug-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Marcus Merchasin'S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery Requests, Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-04-2017 Continued To Apr-11-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Marcus Merchasin's motion to compel further discovery responses and for monetary sanctions is continued to April 11, 2017. On its own motion, the court continues the hearing another week to give parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts. Per the March 30 email from Mr. Fellner, it appears that counsel for Mr. Kupfer did not participate in meet and confer efforts as required by this court's tentative ruling issued in advance of the March 28 hearing. If counsel for Mr. Kupfer was not able to comply with that tentative ruling, counsel's obligation was to contest the tentative ruling. Failing to contest the tentative ruling resulted in the tentative ruling becoming an order of the court, which counsel for Mr. Kupfer did not comply with. Giving counsel for Mr. Kupfer the benefit of the doubt, another continuance is provided. No further benefits of the doubt will be accorded to counsel for Mr. Kupfer on this issue. The meet and confer must occur no later than April 6. No later than April 7 counsel for Mr. Merchasin should send a revised report by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for Mr. Kupfer stating, without argument, what issues remain in dispute. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/HK)

Defendant Marcus Merchasin'S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery Requests, Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Continued To Apr-04-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Marcus Merchasin's Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Responses To Written Discovery Requests, Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions is continued to April 4, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. The court is unable to determine which discovery requests which Mr. Merchasin sought further responses remain in dispute and whether the parties have made any further meet and confer efforts after the truncated efforts that were made before the motion was filed. On its own motion, the court continues the hearing to April 4, 2017 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts and for counsel for Mr. Merchasin to provide a brief report stating what issues remain in dispute. No later than March 30 the parties are required to meet and confer on all issues that are still unresolved. No later than March 31 counsel for Mr. Merchasin is required to send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for plaintiff Konstantin Kupfer stating, without argument, all issues raised by this motion that remain in dispute. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
